en:
My aunt is a very famous actress in our country.
She has made more than twenty films, and many of them have been successful.
Have you ever seen any of her films?
She has also travelled abroad and has worked with famous directors in America.
Millions of people know her face, but at home she is just my quiet aunt.
She has won three big prizes, yet she has never become proud.
Last summer she taught me how to speak in front of a camera.
I have always wanted to be an actress like her.
cn:
我姑姑是我们国家一位很有名的女演员。
她已经拍了二十多部电影，其中许多都很成功。
你看过她的任何一部电影吗？
她还去过国外，在美国和著名导演合作过。
数百万人认得她的脸，但在家里她只是我安静的姑姑。
她赢得过三个大奖，却从未变得骄傲。
去年夏天她教我如何在镜头前说话。
我一直都想成为像她那样的女演员。

en:
On the motorway a fast car is more dangerous than a slow one.
"How fast can we go, Dad?" my little brother asked.
"There is a speed limit here," Father said, "and we are only allowed to drive at seventy miles an hour."
A sports car went past us much faster than that.
"That driver is not being careful," Father said quietly.
"You must always keep inside the limit, even when the road is empty."
A wider road is safer, but you mustn't drive too fast on it.
So Father drove more slowly than the other cars and we arrived safely.
cn:
在高速公路上，一辆快车比一辆慢车更危险。
“爸爸，我们能开多快？”我的小弟弟问道。
“这里有速度限制，”爸爸说，“我们只被允许以每小时七十英里的速度行驶。”
一辆跑车比那快得多地从我们身边驶过。
“那个司机开车不小心，”爸爸小声说。
“你必须始终保持在限速之内，哪怕路上空无一人。”
更宽的路更安全，但你也不准在上面开得太快。
于是爸爸开得比其他车都慢，我们安全地到达了。

en:
"We are going to have a wonderful holiday," my husband said happily.
"I am going to take my camera, my books, my thick coat and my new shoes."
He put more and more things beside the suitcase.
"Don't be so sure you can carry all that," I said.
"The suitcase is already full and far too heavy."
"Don't worry," he answered, "I am going to pack everything tonight."
An hour later he was still trying to close the lid.
"Don't take so much next time!" I said, and we both laughed.
cn:
“我们要过一个美妙的假期，”我丈夫高兴地说。
“我打算带上相机、书、厚外套和新鞋。”
他把越来越多的东西放到手提箱旁边。
“别那么肯定你都能带得动，”我说。
“手提箱已经满了，而且太重了。”
“别担心，”他回答，“我今晚会把所有东西都装好。”
一个小时后，他还在努力合上箱盖。
“下次别带那么多！”我说，我们俩都笑了。

en:
"Have you heard the news?" Tom whispered in the corner of the office.
"They say the boss is going to sell the whole company!"
"That is really sensational news," Mary said. "What will happen to us?"
"I am meeting the boss this afternoon," Tom answered, "so I will ask him myself."
"I think a new company will buy us," said another man.
"Then everything will change next month."
"Nobody knows what will really happen," Mary said.
"We are all coming to work on Monday as usual, so let's just wait and see."
cn:
“你听说那消息了吗？”汤姆在办公室角落里小声说。
“据说老板打算把整个公司卖掉！”
“那真是爆炸性新闻，”玛丽说。“我们会怎么样呢？”
“我今天下午要见老板，”汤姆回答，“所以我会亲自问他。”
“我想会有一家新公司把我们买下，”另一个人说。
“那样的话下个月一切都会改变。”
“谁也不知道究竟会发生什么，”玛丽说。
“我们周一都照常来上班，所以就等着瞧吧。”

en:
Here is the latest report from our news team.
Late last night two thieves broke into a shop in the city centre.
A neighbour heard a noise and quickly telephoned the police.
The police arrived within ten minutes and surrounded the building.
One thief ran into the street, but an officer soon caught him.
The other man hid behind some boxes, yet the police found him too.
They did not take any money away, because the shop alarm frightened them.
Yesterday a judge sent both men to prison for two years.
cn:
以下是本新闻组的最新报道。
昨天深夜，两名小偷闯入了市中心的一家商店。
一位邻居听到了响声，迅速打电话报了警。
警察在十分钟内赶到，包围了那栋建筑。
一名小偷跑到了街上，但一名警官很快抓住了他。
另一个人躲在一些箱子后面，可警察也找到了他。
他们没能拿走任何钱，因为商店的警报器吓到了他们。
昨天，一名法官判处这两个人两年监禁。

en:
On Sunday morning I found my little brother asleep on the sofa.
He was smiling, so I knew he was having a pleasant dream.
When I woke him up, he told me all about it.
"I was flying over the mountains like a bird," he said.
"The sun was shining and warm winds were carrying me higher and higher."
"Below me people were waving and children were laughing."
"Then you woke me up, and I was still flying!"
We both wished the dream had lasted a little longer.
cn:
星期天早晨，我发现小弟弟在沙发上睡着了。
他正微笑着，所以我知道他在做一个美梦。
当我把他叫醒时，他把梦里的一切都告诉了我。
“我像一只鸟一样飞过群山，”他说。
“太阳照耀着，温暖的风把我托得越来越高。”
“下面的人们在挥手，孩子们在欢笑。”
“然后你把我叫醒了，那时我还在飞呢！”
我们俩都希望那个梦能再持续久一点。

en:
"Hello, is that you, John? Peter speaking."
"Yes, it's me. I'm so glad you have rung at last!"
"Have you been busy recently? I haven't seen you for weeks."
"I have just returned from a long trip to the north."
"I visited three cities and met many old friends there."
"Have you seen Mary lately?" John asked.
"I saw her last Sunday, but she hasn't heard your news yet."
"Then I will call her tonight," John said happily.
cn:
“喂，是你吗，约翰？我是彼得。”
“是的，是我。你终于打来电话，我太高兴了！”
“你最近一直很忙吗？我好几周没见到你了。”
“我刚从北方的一次长途旅行回来。”
“我去了三座城市，还在那里见到了许多老朋友。”
“你最近见过玛丽吗？”约翰问。
“我上周日见过她，不过她还没听说你的消息。”
“那我今晚给她打电话，”约翰高兴地说。

en:
Last Saturday little Sally had her first train ride.
She was very excited and sat close to the window all the way.
The train moved quickly, and green fields were rushing past outside.
"Look, Mummy, cows are eating grass in that field!" she cried.
Soon the train crossed a wide river, and boats were sailing on the water.
Her mother pointed at a small village and told her its name.
Sally saw farms, bridges and hills, and she asked a hundred questions.
When they arrived, she said it was the best day of her life.
cn:
上周六，小萨莉第一次坐了火车。
她非常兴奋，一路上都紧挨着窗户坐着。
火车开得很快，绿色的田野在窗外飞驰而过。
“看，妈妈，那片田里有牛在吃草！”她喊道。
很快，火车驶过一条宽阔的河，河面上有船在航行。
她妈妈指着一个小村庄，告诉她那村子的名字。
萨莉看到了农场、桥梁和小山，她问了上百个问题。
到站时，她说这是她一生中最美好的一天。

en:
Last Sunday our family went for a walk through the woods.
A narrow path led us in among the tall old trees.
The children were running ahead and picking up bright red leaves.
My little sister was chasing a butterfly across the grass.
While we were talking quietly, the sky suddenly grew dark.
It began to rain hard, and cold drops fell through the branches.
We all laughed and ran back along the path to the car.
By the time we reached home, the sun was shining again.
cn:
上周日，我们全家到树林里散步。
一条窄路把我们引进了高大的老树之间。
孩子们跑在前面，捡着鲜红的树叶。
我的小妹妹正追着一只蝴蝶穿过草地。
就在我们轻声聊天的时候，天空突然暗了下来。
开始下起大雨，冰凉的雨点透过树枝落下来。
我们都笑着，沿着小路跑回了车里。
等我们到家时，太阳又出来了。
